skill.name: box-automation
skill.description: Automate Box cloud storage operations including file upload/download, search, folder management, sharing, collaborations, and metadata queries via Rube MCP (Composio). Always search tools first for...
skill.summary: Automate Box cloud storage operations via Rube MCP, including file transfers, search, folder management, and collaboration features.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 255 static findings are false positives. The skill is documentation-only (SKILL.md markdown file) with no executable code. Detected 'external_commands' patterns are backtick-enclosed tool names like `BOX_UPLOAD_FILE` and `RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS` in documentation examples, not actual Ruby shell execution. 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' detections are string references to ID '0' (root folder) in documentation. 'Hardcoded URL' findings are legitimate Box URL examples showing how to extract IDs. No prompt injection attempts or malicious intent detected.

low_findings.title: False Positive: External Commands Pattern in Documentation
low_findings.description: Static scanner detected 238 'external_commands' patterns flagged as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. However, SKILL.md is a markdown documentation file (not Ruby code). The backticks enclose API tool names like `BOX_UPLOAD_FILE`, `RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS`, `BOX_GET_FOLDER_INFORMATION` for documentation formatting. These are inline code references, not executable shell commands. No actual code execution occurs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Verified file is markdown documentation, not executable code. Backticks are standard markdown for inline code samples, not shell execution.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Hardcoded URLs in Documentation
low_findings.description: Static scanner detected 4 'Hardcoded URL' patterns at lines 22, 55, 168, 169. These are legitimate Box web app URL examples (https://*.app.box.com/files/123) showing users how to extract file/folder IDs from the browser address bar. This is educational documentation, not suspicious network activity.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs are clearly marked as examples in documentation for ID extraction, not suspicious endpoints or data exfiltration.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Detection
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 6 instances of 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' at lines 3, 45, 74, 104, 107, 238. These are string references to Box API's root folder identifier '0' (e.g., 'use "0" for root folder'), not cryptographic operations. The scanner incorrectly associated ID '0' with weak cryptography.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Context confirms '0' is a folder ID string, not a cryptographic reference. Documentation explicitly explains 'Root folder: Always ID "0"'.

content.value_statement: Manual file management in Box is slow and error-prone. This skill automates uploads, downloads, search, and folder operations through Rube MCP integration.
actual_capabilities: Upload files to Box folders and download files by ID
Search Box content by name, type, metadata, and date filters
Create, update, move, copy, and delete folders
Manage file sharing, collaborations, and access permissions
Query files and folders by metadata templates
List and manage Box Sign requests
limitations: Requires Rube MCP server connection and Box OAuth setup before use
Box Sign features require enterprise account with Sign enabled
Files over 50MB need chunk upload APIs not covered by standard tools
Cannot copy or delete root folder (ID '0')
Rate limits apply per Box API endpoint

use_cases.title: Automated Document Archiving
use_cases.description: Upload completed project documents from local folders to specified Box folders for archival retention.

use_cases.title: Content Audit and Cleanup
use_cases.description: Search Box for files matching criteria, list folder contents, and identify old files for deletion.

use_cases.title: Client Collaboration Setup
use_cases.description: Create shared folders, set up collaboration invites with appropriate roles, and configure access permissions.

prompt_templates.title: Upload Files to Box
prompt_templates.prompt: Upload the file at /path/to/document.pdf to Box folder ID 123456. Name it 'Client Contract v2.pdf' in Box.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to upload a single file to a known Box folder

prompt_templates.title: Search and Download
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Box for files named 'Q4 Report' in folder 987654. Download the first matching file.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to find a specific file and download it

prompt_templates.title: Create Folder Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a folder structure in Box: '2025/Projects/Alpha' under parent folder 111222. Set up shared links with open access.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to organize files with nested folders

prompt_templates.title: Manage Collaborations
prompt_templates.prompt: List all collaborations on file 456789. Update user@example.com from viewer to editor role.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to change access permissions for collaborators

output_examples.output: Successfully uploaded contract.pdf to Box. File ID: 789012. View at https://app.box.com/file/789012
Found 5 files matching 'presentations': 1) Q4_Slides.pptx (ID: 111, updated 2 days ago), 2) Sales_Deck.pdf (ID: 222, updated 1 week ago), 3) Training.pptx (ID: 333, updated 2 weeks ago)

best_practices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to verify available tools and current schemas before operations
Use marker-based pagination for large datasets instead of offset (limited to 10000)
Extract file and folder IDs from Box web URLs by copying the numeric portion from the address bar
anti_patterns: Do not assume connection is active without checking RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS status first
Avoid using offset beyond 10000 in search queries as the API rejects these requests
Never attempt to delete non-empty folders without setting recursive: true parameter

faq.question: How do I get started with Box automation?
faq.answer: Add Rube MCP server URL to your client configuration, call RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS with toolkit 'box', complete OAuth if prompted, then verify ACTIVE status.

faq.question: Where do I find folder and file IDs?
faq.answer: Copy the numeric portion from Box web URLs. For https://app.box.com/files/123456, the file ID is '123456'. Root folder is always '0'.

faq.question: Can I upload large files?
faq.answer: Standard tools support files up to 50MB. Larger files require chunk upload APIs which are not available through the standard Rube MCP Box toolkit.

faq.question: Why did my search return no results?
faq.answer: Check that query or mdfilters parameter is provided. Verify ancestor_folder_ids are correct. Boolean operators must be uppercase (AND, OR, NOT).

faq.question: What happens when I delete a folder?
faq.answer: BOX_DELETE_FOLDER moves items to trash by default. Use BOX_PERMANENTLY_REMOVE_FOLDER for permanent deletion. Non-empty folders require recursive: true.

faq.question: Can I share files with external users?
faq.answer: Yes, use BOX_UPDATE_FILE or BOX_UPDATE_FOLDER to create shared links. Use BOX_LIST_FILE_COLLABORATIONS and BOX_UPDATE_COLLABORATION to manage specific user access.
